sections.paragraph: m. a column, from the root צוּק i.q. יָצַק in Hiphil הִצִּיק to set up. Well explained by Kimchi, עַמּוּד, מַעֲמָד. 1Sa 2:8, מְצֻקֵי אֶרֶץ “the columns of the earth,” i.q. עַמּוּרֵי אֶרֶץ. Used figuratively of an abrupt lofty rock, like a column, 1Sa 14:5, “the fore-front of the one מָצוּק מִצָּפֹון מוּל פּ׳ (is) a column (or abrupt rock) northward, over against Michmash.” (The Talmudists use צוּק for a lofty and steep mountain. The word which some have compared, طاق a lofty mountain, is not of Phœnicio-Shemitic origin, and ought not to be referred to this place.)
